targets.rawSql: SELECT count() AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data
SELECT count() AS value FROM github_analyzer.repo_metadata FINAL
SELECT count() AS value FROM github_analyzer.repo_metadata_history

SELECT round(100.0 * history_rows / greatest(events_rows, 1), 2) AS value FROM (SELECT (SELECT count() FROM github_analyzer.github_data) AS events_rows, (SELECT count() FROM github_analyzer.repo_metadata_history) AS history_rows)
SELECT max(created_at) AS value FROM github_analyzer.github_data
SELECT max(snapshot_at) AS value FROM github_analyzer.repo_metadata_history

SELECT repo_name, count() AS snapshots, max(stars) AS latest_stars, max(snapshot_at) AS last_seen FROM github_analyzer.repo_metadata_history WHERE snapshot_at >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR GROUP BY repo_name ORDER BY snapshots DESC, latest_stars DESC LIMIT 12

SELECT repo_name, max(stars) - min(stars) AS star_delta, min(stars) AS min_stars, max(stars) AS max_stars, max(snapshot_at) AS last_seen FROM github_analyzer.repo_metadata_history WHERE snapshot_at >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR GROUP BY repo_name HAVING count() > 1 ORDER BY star_delta DESC, max_stars DESC LIMIT 12

options.content:
### Fast drilldown
- Use `API Request Logs` to search by `request_id` or `trace_id`.
- Clicking the derived `TraceID` field opens the same trace in Tempo.
- `Largest Star Delta` proves history snapshots are being appended over time, not overwritten.
- If infra panels go blank, check `Observability Stack Health` first: it reflects Prometheus scrape reality, not expected services.
